# Chat With Your Data (CWYD) — Repository Instructions

These instructions are **always loaded**. Read them before doing anything else.

## Repository layout (truth)

- `code/` — **v1 (legacy)**. Flask + Streamlit + Semantic Kernel + Prompt Flow + direct Azure OpenAI SDK. **Do not extend.** Only touch v1 to perform documented removals listed in [v2/docs/development_plan.md](../v2/docs/development_plan.md) §2.1.
- `v2/` — **v2 (active development)**. FastAPI + LangGraph + Agent Framework + Foundry IQ. All new work goes here.
- `infra/` (root) — v1 Bicep. v2 Bicep lives in `v2/infra/`.
- `tests/` (root) — v1 e2e/integration. v2 tests live under `v2/tests/` and `v2/src/**/tests/`.

## Mandatory references — consult before editing

1. [v2/docs/development_plan.md](../v2/docs/development_plan.md) — phase ordering, file paths, scope, removals, additions. Source of truth for **what** to build and **when**. **Always read §0 Status snapshot + the head of §0.1 debt queue before proposing a task.** As of 2026-05-11 the **active phase is Phase 6 — Functions blueprints / modular RAG indexing pipeline** (`batch_start`, `batch_push`, `add_url`, `search_skill` under `v2/src/functions/`; ingestion-only extensions under `v2/src/functions/core/`). Phases 1–5 + 5.5 are closed; #35d (FE admin merge), #35g (per-tenant overrides), and #24 partial (FE SSE polish) are open and explicitly **non-blocking** for Phase 6.
2. [v2/docs/pillars_of_development.md](../v2/docs/pillars_of_development.md) — every new core element must declare its pillar (Stable Core, Scenario Pack, Configuration Layer, or Customization Layer) in the file/class docstring. **Read-only product policy** — never edited by agents.
3. Repo memory `cwyd-tech-stack.md` — current stack truth (versions, services, package managers, test counts).

## External pattern sources — read-only

You may **fetch and read** these for architectural patterns. **Never copy code wholesale** — adapt with attribution in a code comment when the pattern is non-trivial.

- Content Generation Solution Accelerator: <https://github.com/microsoft/content-generation-solution-accelerator>
- Multi-Agent Custom Automation Engine (MACAE): <https://github.com/microsoft/Multi-Agent-Custom-Automation-Engine-Solution-Accelerator>

Specifically:

- MACAE → managed-identity + RBAC + no-Key-Vault env-var pattern; agent-to-agent message bus; SSE streaming pattern.
- CGSA → React/Vite + FastAPI plug-and-play surface; admin merged into frontend; reasoning visualization patterns.

## Hard rules

0. **Step 0 — sync agent guidance before any change.** Before any reorganization, refactor, or error fix, first read the agent instructions and prompt files that scope the change (this file + the relevant `.github/instructions/v2-*.instructions.md`). If the guidance is stale, contradicts the requested change, or is silent on a decision the change implies, **propose an instruction update first and wait for user approval** before touching code or other docs. Out-of-date guidance is the root cause of re-work, throwing away decisions, and re-introducing removed concepts.
1. **One unit per turn.** Implement exactly **one class** OR **one method** per implementation turn. No "and while I'm here…" edits. Do not create multiple files of production code in a single turn unless they are a class + its test stub.
2. **Test-first contract.** Every new method/class lands with at least a `pytest` (Python) or `vitest`/`jest` (TS) test file in the same turn. Tests must execute (pass or fail with a clear assertion), not just exist.
3. **Pillar declaration.** Every new module/class in `v2/src/**` opens with a docstring header:
   ```
   Pillar: <Stable Core | Scenario Pack | Configuration Layer | Customization Layer>
   Phase: <1..7 from development_plan.md>
   ```
   The pillars file (`v2/docs/pillars_of_development.md`) is **read-only product policy** — agents reference it, never edit it. Any proposed change to pillars must be raised with the user as a separate request.
4. **Plug-and-play via registry.** Backend must run headless (no frontend dependency). Frontend must accept `VITE_BACKEND_URL` and consume the generated OpenAPI client. **All swappable concerns** (credentials, llm, embedders, parsers, search, chat_history, orchestrators) live under `v2/src/backend/core/providers/<domain>/` and self-register via `@registry.register("key")` against the generic `Registry[T]` in `v2/src/backend/core/registry.py`. Caller code does `domain.create(key, ...)` — never `if/elif` provider dispatch, never lazy in-function imports of provider classes.
5. **Multi-agent ready.** All orchestrators implement the same async interface (`v2/src/backend/core/providers/orchestrators/base.py`). All emit events on the typed reasoning channel — never bury reasoning inside the answer string.
6. **Reasoning feed.** SSE channels: `reasoning`, `tool`, `answer`, `citation`, `error`. The frontend renders `reasoning` in a collapsible panel; o-series output flows there.
7. **No banned tech / no removed features.** Do not introduce: Streamlit, Prompt Flow, Semantic Kernel, Poetry, direct `openai`/`AzureOpenAI` SDK calls in v2, Key Vault for app secrets, **one-click "Deploy to Azure" ARM button** (v2 is `azd`-only), Azure Bot Service, Teams extension. The full removal list lives in [v2/docs/development_plan.md](../v2/docs/development_plan.md) §2.1 and is binding — never re-add a removed feature. Use Foundry IQ + RBAC + Managed Identity.
8. **Every phase ends green.** `azd up` (or local `docker compose up`) must succeed at the end of each phase. Never leave a half-wired phase.
9. **Clean, modular, plug-and-play — anchored to dev_plan + pillars.** v1 is spaghetti and is being *replaced*, never imitated. Every addition (file, package, container, sidecar, abstraction, config format) must cite (a) a task # in [v2/docs/development_plan.md](../v2/docs/development_plan.md) §3.4 or §4, **and** (b) a pillar in [v2/docs/pillars_of_development.md](../v2/docs/pillars_of_development.md). v1 (`code/`, `docker/`, `infra/`) is a reference *only* for §2.1 removals; do not copy v1 patterns. CGSA/MACAE are read-only architectural references — adapt, never wholesale-copy. Defaults: one runtime per container, no invented sidecars, no premature abstractions, plug-and-play preserved (backend-only and frontend-only profiles must both boot).
10. **Ask before changing structure.** Any new top-level folder, new package in `pyproject.toml`/`package.json`, new module layout under `v2/src/**`, or rename across the tree requires explicit user confirmation **before** the planner emits a Work Order or the implementer touches a file. Structural decisions are not for agents to make unilaterally.
11. **Naming-convention stability across languages.** Once a symbol is named in source code, do not rename it casually — renames break diffs, history, and downstream references. Conventions (always followed; never invent a third style):
    - **Bicep** — `camelCase` for params, vars, modules, resources, outputs (e.g. `containerAppsEnvName`, `acaWorkloadProfileName`). Module symbolic names are `camelCase` nouns (`backendContainerApp`). Hoist any string literal repeated 2+ times to a `var` at the top of the section that uses it.
    - **Python** — `snake_case` for functions, methods, variables, modules; `PascalCase` for classes; `UPPER_SNAKE_CASE` for module-level constants; `_leading_underscore` for private. Match PEP 8 exactly. **Closed-set string literals must be `enum.StrEnum`, not module constants.** Whenever ≥2 related string literals form a closed set (type discriminators, modes, channels, status values, sibling partition keys), define `class Foo(StrEnum)` (Python 3.11+) and reference members instead of bare `_FOO = "foo"` / `_BAR = "bar"` constants. `StrEnum` subclasses `str`, so wire serialization (JSON, SQL params, dict keys) is unchanged and round-trip equality with raw strings still holds. Single-value constants (URLs, SQL templates, lone sentinels with no siblings) stay as `UPPER_SNAKE_CASE`. Pydantic `Literal[...]` *type annotations* on model fields are not affected — they are types, not runtime values. **Registry-driven carve-out (added 2026-06-02 for entry-point discovery).** When a settings field is typed as a `StrEnum` whose members are *first-party* keys of a `Registry[T]` provider domain (Hard Rule #4 dispatch surface) and that registry accepts third-party extensions via `backend.core.discovery.load_entry_points("cwyd.providers.<domain>")` at app startup, the field type **MAY** be widened to `<MyEnum> | str` so an operator can select a third-party-registered key via env var. Discipline: (a) the `StrEnum` still defines the *well-known first-party set* and every internal branch / comparison uses `MyEnum.MEMBER` — the `str` arm exists **only** so Pydantic accepts unknown registered keys; (b) validation happens at the registry boundary — `<domain>_registry.registry.get(value)` raises `KeyError` listing every registered key (first-party + third-party); (c) branches that fan out on the field membership use `value in {MyEnum.A, MyEnum.B}` (set-of-enum-members) — never a chain of equalities against bare strings; (d) closed-set fields that **do not** flow through `Registry.get` dispatch (env discriminators, SSE channels, status enums, partition keys, anything the codebase fully controls and that has no plugin extension point) remain hard `StrEnum`-only with **no `str` arm** — the carve-out applies *only* to settings fields whose values are registry keys. Today's registry-driven fields under this carve-out: `DatabaseSettings.db_type: DbType | str` (registry: `providers.databases`), `SearchSettings.index_store: IndexStore | str` (registry: `providers.search`). Future registry-driven settings inherit the same widening **without re-amending this rule** — the carve-out is structural, not field-by-field. **Types are always available at runtime — `if TYPE_CHECKING:` and `from __future__ import annotations` are banned in `v2/`.** All imports go in the regular import block; all annotations resolve to real symbols at class-definition time. No string-quoted forward references — use `typing.Self` (PEP 673) for self-refs, and import every type a module annotates with. Rationale: lazy / quoted annotations created two recurring failure modes during cleanup audit batch 2 — (a) silent drift where the runtime symbol disappeared but the string annotation kept type-checking green; (b) Pydantic v2 + LangGraph wiring that introspects `__annotations__` at runtime and crashes on unresolved forward refs. **No exceptions.** If a genuine circular import surfaces (the only legitimate historical use case), fix it structurally by extracting the shared type to a leaf module (e.g. `v2/src/backend/core/types.py` or a new `v2/src/backend/core/contracts/`) — never reach for the guard. Such an extraction is a structural change and triggers Hard Rule #10 (ask the user first). The v2-wide invariant is enforced by `v2/tests/shared/test_no_type_checking_or_future_annotations.py` (AST walk over every `*.py` under `v2/`). Static type-checker enforcement is wired via CI (`pyright --strict` for `v2/src/backend/**` + `v2/src/functions/core/**`; see [v2/docs/development_plan.old.md](../v2/docs/development_plan.old.md) §0.1 Q13 for the original wiring trail; the live status tracker lives in [v2/docs/development_plan.md](../v2/docs/development_plan.md) §2.3). **`Any` only at boundaries.** `pyright --strict` is configured on `v2/src/backend/**` + `v2/src/functions/core/**`; CI fails on any drift from `0 errors / 0 warnings / 0 information`. Explicit `Any` annotations are permitted **only** at three boundary classes: (a) third-party SDK responses whose shape we deliberately don't narrow (`dict[str, Any]` rows from `asyncpg`/`cosmosdb`/`azure_search`, openai/agent-framework Protocol stubs returning `Any`); (b) Pydantic `metadata: dict[str, Any]` fields on `Chunk` / `*Event` / `ConversationRequest` etc. where extensibility is the design; (c) `**kwargs: Any` on SDK Protocol stubs only — **never** in internal plumbing. Every `cast(...)` and every `# pyright: ignore[...]` / `# type: ignore[...]` must either (i) have an inline comment naming the SDK boundary it crosses, or (ii) map to a tracked debt row in [v2/docs/development_plan.md](../v2/docs/development_plan.md) §0.1. See also Hard Rule #15 (closed-set dict returns must be typed models) — together #11 and #15 are the type-discipline pair: #11 governs *closed-set string literals* (use `StrEnum`) and *internal plumbing* (no `Any`); #15 governs *closed-set dict returns* (use a `BaseModel`). The same boundary carve-outs (third-party SDK responses, externally-defined protocol payloads) apply to both.
    - **TypeScript** — `camelCase` for variables, functions, methods; `PascalCase` for types, interfaces, classes, React components; `UPPER_SNAKE_CASE` for module-level constants; no `I`-prefix on interfaces. File names: `camelCase.ts` for utilities, `PascalCase.tsx` for components.
    - **Cross-cutting** — environment variables are `UPPER_SNAKE_CASE` and prefixed (`AZURE_*`, `VITE_*`, `CWYD_*`). Azure resource names follow `<type-abbrev>-<solutionSuffix>` per `v2/infra/abbreviations.json`. Names of public APIs (HTTP routes, OpenAPI operationIds, SSE event types, OrchestratorEvent fields) require user confirmation to rename once shipped.
12. **No mid-phase back-fills.** Debt items discovered while working on Phase N are appended to [v2/docs/development_plan.md](../v2/docs/development_plan.md) §0.1 Debt Queue, **not implemented inline**. The queue is cleared in a single dedicated audit turn at the end of each phase. Within a phase, tasks execute in numeric order from §4 — no "and while we're here" additions, no out-of-order pulls from later phases. The only exception: a current task literally cannot proceed without the missed item; in that case a single back-fill turn is allowed and must be annotated in §0.1 with reason.
13. **`__init__.py` is a package marker.** The **only** permitted content is the module docstring (with the `Pillar:` / `Phase:` header). **Forbidden:** class/function definitions, instantiations (no `registry = Registry(...)`), `__all__` lists, re-export imports (`from .x import Y`), side-effect imports (`from . import concrete_a`), module-level constants. Anything that needs that location (the `Registry[...]` instance for a provider domain, the eager imports that fire `@registry.register(...)`, genuine domain helpers like `credentials.select_default`) lives in a sibling module — by convention `registry.py` for provider domains. Callers import the submodule explicitly: `from backend.core.providers.<domain> import registry as <domain>_registry`, then `<domain>_registry.registry.get(key)(**kwargs)`. **No `create(key, **kwargs)` sugar** — it added zero behavior over `Registry.get` and was removed on 2026-05-20. Enforced by `v2/tests/shared/test_init_files_are_marker_only.py` (AST walk; only allowed top-level node is `ast.Expr(ast.Constant(str))`). Rationale + migration plan + per-domain ledger live in [v2/docs/development_plan.md](../v2/docs/development_plan.md) §2.4 — that section is the single source of truth for this invariant.
14. **SDK boundary resilience.** Every external SDK call (`AzureError` umbrella for Azure SDKs, `openai.APIError`, `asyncpg.PostgresError`, `httpx.HTTPError`, `azure.core.exceptions.HttpResponseError`) crossed inside a provider, pipeline, tool, router, or Functions blueprint **must** be wrapped in `try/except <SDK error umbrella>` with three obligations: (a) `logger.exception(...)` with **structured** `extra={"operation": "<verb>", "provider": "<key>", ...domain-specific snake_case keys}`; (b) **re-raise** the original exception (or wrap in a domain error that preserves `__cause__`); (c) never silently swallow — silent excepts are forbidden under all conditions and enforced by `v2/tests/shared/test_no_silent_excepts.py`. Cleanup paths (`finally`, `async with __aexit__`) own resource release; they do not own error reporting. Status checks (health, diagnostics) classify failures as `pass | degraded | fail` per `v2/src/backend/models/health.py` and never let the SDK exception escape the router. Functions triggers have type-specific contracts: queue triggers return `None` and re-raise so the host's retry → poison policy engages (`@log_queue_errors` in `v2/src/functions/core/`); HTTP triggers always return `func.HttpResponse` via `@map_function_exceptions` which owns the `ValidationError` → 422 / `AzureError` → 502 / `Exception` → 500 ladder. See [.github/instructions/v2-backend-core.instructions.md](./instructions/v2-backend-core.instructions.md) §Resilience for the canonical code pattern.
15. **Closed-set dict returns must be typed models.** When a function returns a dict (or `list[dict]`) whose keys are a **fixed, known-at-author-time schema**, define a Pydantic v2 `BaseModel` (frozen, `extra="forbid"`) and return *that*, not `dict[str, object]` / `dict[str, Any]`. Wire-shape conversion happens **at the SDK boundary** via `model.model_dump(...)` — the model is the source of truth for the schema, the dict is a transport detail. Trigger heuristic: "if you can write the keys as a literal at the call site, the keys are fixed" — `{"id": chunk.id, "content": chunk.content, "title": chunk.source, "content_vector": vector}` is a struct; `{header_name: header_value for header_name in dynamic_headers}` is a dict. **Even arity 1 earns a model** — there is no "one field doesn't need a model" carve-out, because that door always becomes "two fields don't either." **Boundary exceptions (no model required):** (a) third-party SDK responses — `dict[str, Any]` rows from `asyncpg`, `azure.cosmos`, `azure.search.documents` per Hard Rule #11(a); (b) externally-defined protocol payloads whose shape is owned by another party — Easy Auth `x-ms-client-principal` claims blob, JWT payloads, OpenAPI extension dicts; (c) stdlib / SDK structural arguments typed `Mapping[str, object]` — `logging.Logger.<level>(..., extra=...)`, `azure.functions.HttpResponse(headers=...)` — when the receiver requires a Mapping AND the caller spreads with `**` to merge ad-hoc per-call fields; (d) test capture buffers — `captured: dict[str, object] = {}` accumulators in test stubs are not "returns" and are not covered. Helpers under boundary (c) must either be a single-call inline literal, or a constructor-style helper whose docstring cites this rule + the SDK boundary it crosses. Enforced by `v2/tests/shared/test_no_anonymous_dict_returns.py` (AST walk over `v2/src/**/*.py`): every `FunctionDef.returns` whose annotation matches `dict[str, ...]` (or wrappers like `list[dict[...]]`, `dict[...] | None`) must either appear in the allow-list (registry of `(module_path, function_qualname)` tuples maintained alongside the gate, each with a one-line justification) or trigger a failing test. The allow-list grows only via a tracked debt-queue row in [v2/docs/development_plan.md](../v2/docs/development_plan.md) §0.1.
16. **No process narrative in production code.** Comments and docstrings under `v2/src/**` describe **what the code is** — not how it got there, what unit shipped it, what phase it lives in, or what work lands next. The repo's process state lives in [v2/docs/development_plan.md](../v2/docs/development_plan.md), commit history, and tracked debt rows — not in source files that rot the moment a phase advances. **Forbidden in production-code comments + docstrings:** (a) forward-looking unit IDs and "next-work" pointers (`"U11 lands next"`, `"X lands in Phase 7"`, `"future smoke / e2e tests can assert"`); (b) bare unit IDs in any role (the token `\bU\d+[a-z]?\b` — `U7g`, `U10c` etc. — even inside a Phase header tail, even as a cross-module pointer parenthetical); (b2) dev_plan §4 task numbers (the token `\btask\s+#\d+\b` — `task #20`, `task #41` etc. — even inside a Phase header tail, even as a parenthetical next to a live symbol name); (c) phase roll-forward narrative (`"the Phase 6 blueprint set landed so far"`, `"as of <date>"`, `"so far"` adjacent to `Phase`); (d) dev_plan section citations (`"per [v2/docs/development_plan.md] §4.6.1"` — the dev_plan is the work-tracker, not API docs); (e) self-narrative (`"added in this turn"`, `"removed in CU-009b"`); (f) process date stamps (`"2026-05-28"`, `"REFACTOR-B (2026-05-06)"`) — git blame is the source of truth. **Permitted (carve-outs):** (a) **`Pillar:` / `Phase:` docstring headers** mandated by Hard Rule #3 — but the Phase line carries only `Phase: <N>` plus the phase's standing descriptive name (`"Phase: 6 (Functions blueprints / modular RAG indexing pipeline)"`), never a `, U#` task tag in the tail; (b) **inline `# pyright: ignore[...]` debt citations** carrying a `<DEBT-ID>` token (e.g. `# Debt (dev_plan §0.1 U8i-EMBEDDER-CTOR-DEBT): ...`) — the gate carves out any line containing the substring `-DEBT`, anchoring the comment to a tracked debt row (Hard Rule #11/#15 contract); (c) **standing-policy anchors** (`"per Hard Rule #15"`, `"per Hard Rule #11(a)"`) — these cite live, stable policy that lives in a stable doc, not transient process; (d) **architectural cross-module pointers naming live symbols** (`"Companion to functions.add_url.blueprint"`, `"Consumed by the HTTP trigger in blueprint.py"`) — these describe relationships between symbols that still exist, with no unit-ID tag attached; (e) **Azure SDK API version literals** (`"2024-12-01-preview"`, `"api version 2025-04-01"`) — these are technical content the SDK pins on, not process dates; the gate distinguishes them by the trailing `-preview` / `-ga` suffix or by an `api version` context phrase anywhere in the surrounding docstring or comment block. Enforced by `v2/tests/shared/test_no_process_narrative_in_src.py` (per-file gate; AST-walks docstrings + `tokenize`-walks comments under `v2/src/`, runs a fixed regex set, applies the carve-outs in (b)–(e) at line granularity except for the API version carve-out which is block-scoped). The gate has no growable allow-list — fix the comment, do not exempt the file. If a genuinely new carve-out class surfaces, raise it as a Hard Rule #16 amendment request before adding it to the gate.
17. **All imports at module top.** All `import` / `from X import Y` statements must live at the top of the module, before any non-import statement (the only allowed prelude is the module docstring + the `Pillar:` / `Phase:` header it carries per Hard Rule #3). **Forbidden:** (a) imports inside `def` / `async def` / `class` bodies — no lazy imports of any kind, stdlib or third-party, even for "dev-ergonomics" profile splits (every package the v2 backend or functions can hit is already declared in `pyproject.toml` and installs unconditionally); (b) imports inside module-level `if`/`else` or `try`/`except` branches — no soft-dependency `try/except ImportError` shims, no profile-conditional `if backend_only: import …` branches; (c) `if TYPE_CHECKING:` blocks (already banned by Hard Rule #11; reiterated here so the two import rules read together); (d) `from __future__ import annotations` (also banned by #11). Imports follow PEP 8 grouping (stdlib → third-party → first-party) with a blank line between groups; ordering within a group follows the codebase's existing tooling defaults (ruff/isort). **Circular imports are not a carve-out** — fix them structurally by extracting the shared type/protocol to a leaf module per Hard Rule #11 precedent (`v2/src/backend/core/types.py` or a new per-domain leaf under `v2/src/backend/core/`), then top-import from both call sites. Such an extraction is a structural change and triggers Hard Rule #10 (ask the user first). **Absolute rule with zero carve-outs** — the gate's `_EXEMPTIONS` set is `frozenset()` and stays that way. Enforced by `v2/tests/shared/test_imports_at_top_only.py` (AST walk over every `*.py` under `v2/src/` + `v2/scripts/` + `v2/tests/`; every `ast.Import` / `ast.ImportFrom` node must have `Module` as its enclosing scope AND be preceded in module body only by other imports or by the module docstring). The gate has no growable allow-list — fix the import site, do not exempt the file. Cross-references: Hard Rule #11 (the `TYPE_CHECKING` / `__future__` half of the same import discipline); tracked debt row `IMPORTS-AT-TOP-DEBT` in [v2/docs/development_plan.md](../v2/docs/development_plan.md) §0.1 (records the gate-landing and one-file-per-turn cleanup of pre-existing in-function imports across `v2/tests/`).

## Workflow contract

Use the dedicated agents for any non-trivial v2 work. They enforce the rules above:

- `cwyd-planner` — research + produce a one-unit work order (read-only).
- `cwyd-implementer` — write exactly one unit + test stub.
- `cwyd-tester` — fill the test, run it, report.

For trivial single-line edits, you may proceed directly — but still respect the test-first rule.

## Local development

- Python: `uv sync` from the repo root.
- v2 dev stack: `docker compose -f v2/docker/docker-compose.dev.yml up`.
- v2 backend-only: `docker compose -f v2/docker/docker-compose.dev.yml --profile backend-only up`.
- v2 frontend-only: `docker compose -f v2/docker/docker-compose.dev.yml --profile frontend-only up` (set `VITE_BACKEND_URL`).
- CI validation image: `docker build -f v2/docker/Dockerfile.ci-validate -t cwyd-ci .` then run.

## Session conventions

- **End-of-turn beep is the agent's responsibility.** GitHub Copilot does not execute Claude Code-style `~/.claude/settings.json` `Stop` hooks, so the agent **must** emit the chime as its **final tool call** before handing the conversation back to the user. One call per turn, no exceptions, no duplicates:
  - Windows (default): `run_in_terminal` (sync, ~500ms) with `[console]::beep(880,200); [console]::beep(1175,250)` — two ascending tones.
- **Mid-turn waits for user input require a *different* beep.** Whenever the agent is about to block on the user *before* the turn ends (calling `vscode_askQuestions`, sending an interactive prompt to a terminal via `send_to_terminal`, or otherwise pausing for a human reply mid-task), the agent **must** first emit a single low beep:
  - Windows (default): `run_in_terminal` with `[console]::beep(660,250)` (sync, ~250ms, distinct from the two-tone end-of-turn chime).
- **`[console]::beep` is reserved for these two signals.** Do not call it from any other tool invocation, do not bury it inside another command, and do not skip it when wrapping up.
